title: subagent: transcript store global LazyLock leaks memory — TTL eviction only on insert/load, no periodic sweep
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Performance and Complexity / Memory. Location: src/subagent.rs:346-378 (TRANSCRIPT_STORE, store_transcript, load_transcript). Violation: The TTL eviction loop runs only inside store_transcript and load_transcript. If no agents are spawned or loaded, dead transcripts never evict. Worse, a transcript can be huge (messages Vec<Value> with tool outputs). Hundreds of agents over a long session = bounded only by TTL * creation rate; but the insert path itself iterates the whole HashMap on every store — O(N) per insert. Mandated Refactor: (a) Run eviction on a background tokio::spawn loop with an interval timer. (b) Hard-cap number of stored transcripts (LRU with a max of 50). (c) Hard-cap per-transcript message list size. This is a silent 'agent pool of 10 over 24h' memory explosion.
